rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC, VS. KILLARNEY CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement On Behalf Of Pltfs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 2, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PERFORMING ARTS LLC's Motion For Good Faith Settlement On Behalf Of Plaintiffs. Granted. No opposition filed. The Court will sign the proposed Order submitted with the moving papers, as to which there has been no objection filed. Moving party is requested to prepare a proposed order which makes clear which parties have moved for good faith settlement, and the parties as to whom the good faith finding is made, consistent with the notice of motion. The proposed order submitted with the moving papers is not specific enough, given that there is another motion for good faith settlement on calendar in this case on the same day. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARIE DELGADO VS. BAILEY E DOUGLAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of The Application For Good Faith Settlement; Application
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 2, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTS BAILEY DOUGLAS and JENNIFER OLNEY'S Hearing Of The Application For Good Faith Settlement. Hearing required. No opposition filed. The Court does not have courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. A courtesy copy of the moving papers must be provided to the Court by 9:00 a.m. on January 2, 2013. If possible, moving party is requested to send an electronic copy of the moving papers to contestdept302tr@sftc.org as soon as possible. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: COLLEEN BRAND VS. G7 SOURCE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Disqualify Ptlf'S Counsel Alex Hernaez
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 2, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANTS CRAIG CAUDILL and G7 SOURCE, INC. DBA HALENT LABORATORIES' Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff'S Counsel Alex Hernaez. Denied. Plaintiff has presented evidence that she has given informed, written consent to have Mr. Hernaez represent her. Rule of Professional Conduct 5-210. At this stage in the litigation, Defendants have not shown that Mr. Hernaez's testimony is genuinely needed on the topics described in their opening memorandum, or that Mr. Hernaez's testimony would create a conflict or be prejudicial to his client. Defendants have not shown that Mr. Hernaez's alleged conduct in the course of his representation threatens the integrity to the judicial system or that there is detriment to Defendants such that Plaintiff should be denied her choice of counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MOORE VS. STANLEY BLACK & DECKER, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 2, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE's Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Enter Judgment. Hearing required. Parties are to be prepared to address the "payment procedures" set forth in Consent to Judgment 4.1.1, in particular: what is meant by the title "Funds Moore In Trust;" why payments are to be made to "Thompson Hine LLP;" whether Thompson Hine LLP is the "attorney of record for Stanley" (see 4.1.1) and if so why they have not been served with this motion; and why there appear to be duplicate checks for $35,500 in attorney fees when the requested amount is $35,500, as well as duplicate checks for other fees/penalties. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL BRIMER VS. VANDOR LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 2, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER's Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Enter Judgment. Hearing required. Parties are to be prepared to address the "payment procedures" set forth in Consent to Judgment 4.3., in particular the language in the first paragraph of 4.5.1 ("shall be Brimer in trust"). = (302/MJM)
